Canadian Pacific.


Mr.. and Mrs. JAMESON made q
number Of trips to smaller towns in
Washington and were delighted
with the country. They have made
hosts Of friends during their stay
here. who regret very much to see
them depart.


QUESTS At JACKSON HOME


Mr.. and Mrs. M. T. Jackson Of
1905 Gardner avenue have had as
their guests DR.. and Mrs. M. Matzen
of Columbus, Neb. Mrs. Matzen Is
a niece of Mrs. Jackson.


DR.. Matzen left last evening and
Mrs. Matzen will follow this even
ing. They will make trip to Top
penish and Yakima. Wash, with a
possible view Of making one of the
places their future home.


MRS. REYNOLDS Will SPEAK At


SERIES OF MEETINGS.


Mrs. M. J. Reynolds, the speaker
of the Washington Political Equal.
ity league, has returned from a
short stumping trip in Lincoln
county, where she spoke at Wilbur.
Creston and Mondovi in the inter-
ests of the pending equal suffrage
amendment. She spoke Sunday
night at Opportunity to a crowded
union meeting of the Christian and
Methodist churches.


Mrs. Reynolds will speak next
Friday evening. August IL. in the
Grange hall at Orchard Avenue.
Miss Fay Morvillius will sing. and
Miss Mabel Price will give humor-
Gus readings. Mrs. Reynolds will
speak Saturday afternoon at the big
good government rally at Mantle
park, given by the Farmers union
and the labor unions. She will
speak next Sunday morning at IL at
the Dan Bush Rescue mission, cor
rer of Front and Stevens. when
Miss Morvillius will sing. and on
Sunday evening. the 14th. in the
Christian church at Medical Lake.
